limitations: ai_investment_intensity: TXN is an analog/embedded semiconductor supplier, not an AI-focused builder. The $4.55B capex and $3.94B R&D are not explicitly attributed to AI; they relate to 300mm fab expansion broadly. Data center is named as a market (9% of revenue) but no AI-specific investment dollar is quoted. Score reflects generic AI-adjacent positioning only.

ai_narrative_concreteness: AI is not named as a product or initiative; data center demand is referenced as a growth driver but no AI-specific use case or deployment is described. Score reflects aspirational/market-level language.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section material is present. AI is mentioned specifically as a cybersecurity threat vector (use of AI tools by bad actors), with named risk mechanisms (data compromise, operational disruption). No quantification or formal AI governance body is disclosed beyond CIO/CISO oversight structure, which is framed around cybersecurity broadly.
